jokes.text: Character trying on someone else's costume without permission
jokes.setup: Character asking about a costume being 'badass'
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's not even theirs
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment but lacks character specificity or strong setup

jokes.text: Character not noticing it wasn't his hot coffee
jokes.setup: Character being oblivious about basic things
jokes.punchline: Question about limits to what he won't notice
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven observational humor with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Handbag as answer to seven-letter crossword clue
jokes.punchline: Simple, obvious answer provided
jokes.explanation: Minor workplace humor, lacks punch

jokes.text: Branch on planet Jupiter is up 8,000% in sales
jokes.punchline: Completely impossible location for office branch
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor delivered deadpan in business context

jokes.text: Stanley immediately leaving at 5:00 despite meeting
jokes.punchline: Stanley's strict adherence to leaving time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley character moment - his priorities are clear and unwavering

jokes.text: 'Love you' said to Stanley as he leaves
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly affectionate goodbye to unresponsive Stanley
jokes.explanation: Nice awkward beat but not exceptional

jokes.text: Kevin saying he looks like Michael Moore as his costume statement
jokes.setup: Oscar asking about Kevin's costume statement
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple, literal observation about resembling Michael Moore
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - his straightforward thinking contrasts with Oscar's pretension

jokes.text: Erin answering phone 'How can I haunt you today?'
jokes.punchline: Halloween pun on standard phone greeting
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits Erin's enthusiastic character perfectly

jokes.text: MacGruber countdown interrupting normal conversation
jokes.punchline: Sudden MacGruber bomb defusal routine interruption
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference bit that feels somewhat forced

jokes.text: Coupon book worth $15,000 in savings requiring spending $200,000
jokes.punchline: Mathematical reality of coupon book economics
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about marketing deception that many viewers can relate to

jokes.text: If I was the real Scranton strangler, you'd be so strangled by now
jokes.punchline: Overly helpful explanation of how effective he'd be
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good character voice - the earnestness makes it funnier

jokes.text: To my chickens, I'm the Scranton strangler
jokes.punchline: Creed's matter-of-fact admission about his chickens
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed line - dark, unexpected, economical, and completely in character. Peak Office writing.

jokes.text: Dwight insulting Angela's Olive Oyl costume as his mother
jokes.punchline: Dwight comparing her unfavorably to his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics - Dwight's specific way of insulting fits perfectly

jokes.text: Jim refusing to put on Popeye costume despite Pam's Olive Oyl
jokes.punchline: Jim's resistance to participating
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship dynamic but not particularly strong as comedy

jokes.text: Jim's talking head about being too old for costumes even as a kid
jokes.punchline: Claiming he was too old for costumes even as a child
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Jim's self-aware smugness comes through clearly

jokes.text: MacGruber explaining sales policies with bomb countdown
jokes.setup: Serious business meeting about sales policies
jokes.punchline: Delivering information through MacGruber bomb defusal bit
jokes.explanation: Forced pop culture bit that doesn't integrate well with story

jokes.text: Why don't witches wear panties? To grip the broom
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo punchline
jokes.explanation: Generic dirty joke that could be told anywhere - lacks character specificity

jokes.text: Where does Dracula like to water ski? Lake Erie
jokes.punchline: Eerie/Erie pun
jokes.explanation: Basic pun joke - predictable and not character-specific

jokes.text: Has anyone started calling you Gabe-wad yet?
jokes.punchline: Immediate mocking of his name
jokes.explanation: Good character undermining - highlights Gabe's lack of respect

jokes.text: Darryl hiding ream of paper in shirt thinking he might get hit
jokes.setup: Tense conversation about credit for ideas
jokes.punchline: Darryl's preparation for physical confrontation
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy with character insight - Darryl knows Michael well enough to prepare

jokes.text: Michael's talking head about bringing racists to lunch with Darryl
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate and offensive 'solution' to racism
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael Scott cringe - well-intentioned but completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: Oscar explaining coupon book math while everyone ignores him
jokes.punchline: Everyone's complete disinterest in facts
jokes.explanation: Good Oscar moment - his rationality goes unappreciated as usual

jokes.text: Public School is the name of Danny's bar
jokes.setup: Danny saying he owns a bar
jokes.punchline: Bar name sounds like location description
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that creates brief confusion

jokes.text: Pam choosing whoever smells most like her father
jokes.setup: Discussion about Pam choosing between Jim and Danny
jokes.punchline: Freudian explanation involving father's scent
jokes.explanation: Weird psychological humor but doesn't feel specifically tied to characters

jokes.text: Jim and Pam were Romeo and Juliet except Juliet didn't have a great time
jokes.setup: Everyone treating Jim/Pam/Danny as dramatic love triangle
jokes.punchline: Deconstructing the romantic narrative with reality
jokes.explanation: Sharp writing that punctures romantic comedy tropes with truth

jokes.text: I'm like an elephant in that way. You know what eleph... this sucks for Jim
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to have elephant memory
jokes.punchline: Self-interrupting when he can't remember elephant facts
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment - his self-awareness kicks in at the wrong time

jokes.text: Michael trying to get shy Darryl to come out and embrace being a superstar
jokes.punchline: Darryl's obvious discomfort with attention
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic but not exceptional comedy

jokes.text: Michael being peeved that Darryl went over his head to go behind his back
jokes.punchline: Confusing directional metaphors
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that shows character confusion about office politics

jokes.text: Andy and Kevin telling Danny that Jim and Pam begged them not to go
jokes.setup: Danny inviting people to his party
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing creating more drama
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven awkwardness but not standout

jokes.text: Kevin and Gabe discussing extremes of human physique
jokes.setup: Michael paranoid about people going over his head
jokes.punchline: They're actually discussing their physical differences
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on Michael's paranoia

jokes.text: Kevin teaching Gabe Lady Gaga robot power-down move
jokes.punchline: Kevin just helping with costume moves
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with character dynamics

jokes.text: Danny not remembering if it was two or three dates with Pam
jokes.setup: Trying to minimize their dating history
jokes.punchline: Danny's casual attitude reveals how unmemorable it was
jokes.explanation: Good awkward relationship humor

jokes.text: You can't handle the truth! / Well, that does not sound like me
jokes.setup: Pam explaining Danny never called back
jokes.punchline: Danny's complete lack of self-awareness
jokes.explanation: Good character blindness to own behavior

jokes.text: Suck it
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish response
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective Michael moment - petty and childish

jokes.text: Oscar as rational consumer costume
jokes.punchline: Oscar's meta-commentary costume choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar costume - intellectually superior and judgmental

jokes.text: Michael's Darryl costume with jive talking
jokes.punchline: Offensive stereotypical impression
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Michael - inappropriate and offensive as intended

jokes.text: Monster Mash playing during costume show
jokes.punchline: Obvious song choice for Halloween
jokes.explanation: Generic Halloween atmosphere music - low effort

jokes.text: Angela as sexy nurse costume reluctantly
jokes.setup: Costume contest with Angela's usual conservatism
jokes.punchline: Angela forced into revealing costume
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Angela's discomfort is the joke

jokes.text: Ouija board spelling A-S-S-E-T for Darryl
jokes.punchline: Spelling seems to go toward 'ass' but becomes 'asset'
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that builds tension then resolves positively

jokes.text: Halpert, you looking for someone to bang your wife?
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Danny about not calling Pam back
jokes.punchline: Danny's crude interpretation of Jim's motives
jokes.explanation: Shocking line that cuts through the politeness

jokes.text: Erin bobbing for apples and eating them
jokes.punchline: Erin's enthusiastic participation and consumption
jokes.explanation: Fits Erin's character but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Danny calling Pam dorky as reason for not calling back
jokes.setup: Everyone wanting to know why Danny didn't call
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Harsh truth that creates genuine discomfort - effective cringe

jokes.text: Darryl listing what everyone else did for him vs Michael
jokes.setup: Michael claiming he did things for Darryl
jokes.punchline: Systematic dismantling of Michael's contributions
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation that builds to devastating conclusion about Michael's irrelevance

jokes.text: Michael's hat solution for decision making
jokes.setup: Trying to resolve office hierarchy issues
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical hat-based solution
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael illogical solution but not exceptional

jokes.text: Andy's Cornell a cappella story interrupting Darryl's problems
jokes.punchline: Andy making it about his college experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy self-absorption but could be stronger

jokes.text: Who doesn't call a dork like that back?
jokes.punchline: Jim's protective response using same language
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Jim's loyalty while embracing the 'insult'

jokes.text: Jim and Pam as Popeye and Olive Oyl doing voices
jokes.punchline: Both doing character voices
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup but not particularly strong comedy

jokes.text: Oscar winning costume contest despite rational consumer concept
jokes.setup: Oscar criticizing the contest and prize
jokes.punchline: Him winning the thing he was mocking
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic conclusion to Oscar's arc
